zh.Cruwys Morchard, Devon, England: 英國戴文可盧斯 莫爾哈德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Cruwys於1285年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可盧斯 莫爾哈德時，他的父親Alexander de Cruwys III20歲，母親Constance de London16歲。他於1326年之前與Isabella St Aubin在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1361年在英國戴文可盧斯 莫爾哈德享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cruwys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cruse
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name probably from an unidentified place in Normandy named with Old French crues, crus, creus ‘hollow’. Creuse (in Somme, Picardy) and Cruys-Straëte (formerly in Flanders, now in the French département of Nord), which have the same etymology, are alternative possibilities but seem less likely on geographical and tenurial grounds.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English crus(e) ‘bold, fierce’, although the regular development would be to Crowse (rhyming with house).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Cruwys was born in 1285, in Cruwys Morchard, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander de Cruwys III, was 20 and his mother, Constance de London, was 16. He married Isabella St Aubin before 1326, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1361, in Cruwys Morchard, Devon, England, at the age of 76.

Il a épousé Isabella St Aubin avant 1326, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1361, à Cruwys Morchard, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Cruwys(이)가 1285년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 크루w이스 모르처드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander de Cruwys III(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance de London(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1326년 이전 영국 데폰에서 Isabella St Aubin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1361년 영국 데폰 크루w이스 모르처드에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Cruwys родился в 1285 г. в местности крувис морчард, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alexander de Cruwys III было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance de London, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella St Aubin перед 1326 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1361 г. в местности крувис морчард, девон, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.
